Cytotoxicity test results of KT solutions prepared at differ-
ent concentrations (0.47-0.94-1.88-3.75-7.5-15 mg/mL) for
24 h and 48 h were demonstrated in Fig. 8.
Looking at the results of 24 h and 48 h cytotoxicity tests,
it was seen that cell viability decreases as KT concentration
increases, which means the KT solution caused cell death or
inhibited cell growth in the RG2 cells at higher doses over
time, with a significant difference observed compared to the
control group (100% cell viability). On the other hand, the
difference in cell viability was not significant at lower doses,
except for the 0.47 mg/mL. Also, the cytotoxic effect of KT
solutions after 24 h was found to be greater than observed
after 48 h. This difference may be due to a decrease in the
lethality of the active substance after 24 h, as cell prolifera-
tion increased during the relevant period. Moreover, cyto-
